LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT EDGARDO VIDES DBA E & V RENTALS' MOTION AND SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE ALL CAUSES OF ACTION AS A MERITLESS SLAPP PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 425.16 IS DENIED. THE GRAVAMEN OF THE COMPLAINT IS THE ALLEGED UNINHABITABLE CONDITIONS AND THE UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION IS AT MOST INCIDENTAL. EVEN TO THE EXTENT ANY CAUSE OF ACTION COULD BE CHARACTERIZED AS ARISING OUT OF THE LITIGATION, PLAINTIFF HAS SHOWN A SUFFICIENT LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS. THE PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR FEES IS DENIED. OPPOSING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiff Demand For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Apr-20-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Eric Lifchitz, Atty. for Plaintiff and moving party: Gloria Suncin
No appearances made by Defendant.

RULING: PLAINTIFF GLORIA SUNCIN, MARCELLO MARTINEZ Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiff Demand For Production Of Documents - No opposition was filed. The motion to compel further responses is granted, and full responses are due not later than 15 calendar days from notice of this order. Sanctions in the amount of $1050 to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Moving party has also asked for additional sanctions, but the moving papers do not put the responding party clearly on notice concerning these additional sanctions. Movant's papers confusingly suggest (i) a bar on introduction of document not previously produced both as an alternative to an order requiring their production now, and in addition to that relief; (ii) 'issue' sanctions, but not specified; and finally (iii) movant suggests striking affirmative defenses. It is true that additional sanctions may well be appropriate here, given defendant's failure to comply with an earlier court order, but defendant must be plainly and clearly on notice of the requested sanctions before they are imposed. Issue, evidentiary, and terminating sanctions are available for failures to comply with court orders, and generally willfulness must be established. See generally, 2 CEB, California Civil Discovery Practice 15.91. Failure to comply with this order may in the future result in such sanctions given a proper basis and clear notice to defendant of the requested sanctions(s).

HEARING: Moving party appeared through counsel seeking sanctions in addition to those awarded in tentative order. Tentative adopted without change. Prevailing party to prepare order.
JUDGE CURTIS E.A. KARNOW. NOT REPORTED
